execution_summary: The todo-reference todo-mvp slice reached EM validation_status pass after infra alignment (Bun→Node in Docker, better-sqlite3 rebuild, Next force-dynamic on the page). QA surfaced a low-severity process tension: empty and error flows were well covered at integration/API boundaries but E2E literal assertions (user-visible copy, empty state, retry) were thinner than the architecture brief’s combined integration+E2E expectation. Stitch was waived per delivery; governance artifacts otherwise held. This plan captures governance edits so the next cycle wastes less time reconciling “who must prove what” and avoids repeating native-runtime container pitfalls.
what_worked_well: Clean Architecture + declared coverage scopes in architecture-brief.json gave auditable unit vs integration gates.
Container-only integration discipline matched testing-and-qa-standards and caught persistence/runtime issues early once the image used a Node-compatible toolchain for native addons.
EM→QA handoff with structured validation_status and issues allowed pass with an explicit, low-severity cross-layer note instead of silent drift.
issues_identified: Ambiguity between integration coverage of error paths and E2E obligation for user-visible empty/error/retry states produced last-mile friction in the EM report.
Bun-oriented container base vs better-sqlite3 (native module) forced an unplanned runtime switch—risk of recurrence on future SQLite features if runtime is not pinned in skills.

Next.js static/cache defaults interacting with dynamic server data sometimes require explicit dynamic routing; this was fixed in product code but not reflected in governance guidance for App Router + server actions patterns.
root_causes: testing-and-qa-standards §4 mandates full-feature E2E but does not spell out a crisp split vs integration for empty-state UX vs transport errors, so teams can over-invest in one layer.
Architecture brief names Node SQLite drivers but repo skills did not warn against Bun-only images for native addons in CI/Docker.
quality-gate acceptance templates do not nudge explicit “E2E must assert literal UI states named in brief” vs “integration must assert status codes/mappers”.

improvement_opportunities.rationale: Reduces EM/QA noise when integration proves server-side failure handling but E2E omits literal UI assertions (or the reverse), matching the low-severity issue from this cycle.
improvement_opportunities.proposed_change: In `.cursor/skills/testing-and-qa-standards/SKILL.md`, add a short subsection after §4 (e.g. §4.3) stating: (1) integration scope owns HTTP/Server Action contracts, status codes, and error mapping to application errors; (2) E2E owns every user-visible outcome named in acceptance—empty list states, user-readable error surfaces, and retry/recovery affordances; (3) duplication is allowed but not substitutable—missing E2E proof of a brief-mandated visible state remains a QA finding even if integration lines are covered. Reference stable id `qa-manual-automated-e2e-chromium` and cross-link `integration-tests-full-coverage`.

improvement_opportunities.rationale: Forces a single, labeled consolidation in the engineering_execution_report when QA notes overlap or gaps between layers.
improvement_opportunities.proposed_change: In `.cursor/agents/engineering-manager.md`, under Output Requirements (or Execution Flow near QA completion), add a bullet: when `qa-agent` reports cross-layer test gaps (e.g. integration-heavy error handling vs thin E2E UI assertions), the EM report must tag each issue with `layer: integration` | `e2e` | `both` and point assignees to the testing skill subsection on E2E vs integration responsibilities; do not close the cycle with pass unless severity and residual risk are explicit.

improvement_opportunities.rationale: Prevents repeat of Bun base image + native rebuild surprises for SQLite stacks documented in architecture briefs.
improvement_opportunities.proposed_change: In `.cursor/skills/architecture-standards/SKILL.md`, add a concise bullet under baseline or a new “Runtime / native adapters” note: when the brief selects SQLite via native drivers (e.g. better-sqlite3), CI/Docker images must use a supported Node (or compatible) runtime and libc/musl pairing; document in the brief or infra notes that Bun-only images are a mismatch unless an alternative driver is chosen. Optionally one sentence cross-linking `.cursor/skills/testing-and-qa-standards/SKILL.md` §3 container parity.

non_apply_recommendations.rationale: Per-feature task.json is outside orchestrator apply allowlist; future quality-gate rows can still remove ambiguity early.
non_apply_recommendations.proposed_change: For UI MVPs, add acceptance bullets that name exact E2E-visible checks for empty state and error/retry (strings or ARIA roles) separately from integration checks for API/action failure codes—mirroring architecture-brief constraints_for_implementation.

non_apply_recommendations.rationale: architecture-brief.json is not allowlisted for Step 11; optional future briefs can call out App Router caching defaults when server actions + dynamic lists apply.
non_apply_recommendations.proposed_change: When Server Actions or dynamic todo lists must reflect DB state on every navigation, note in constraints whether routes should opt into dynamic rendering (e.g. `dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` or equivalent) to avoid stale shell issues—so implementation does not rediscover via production bugs.
